manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: olympus, mouse, triangle, horseshoe, film, canada, millionaire, pitch, state.
Your opponent's team words are: pupil, bug, hotel, button, straw, diamond, round, field.
Distractor words are: mail, berlin, opera, club, cap, mine, web, train.
The kill words are: .

message.reasoning:
We need to provide a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words. Must not be any of the words in lists, nor contain parts. Provide clue and list of target words that clue refers to. Could aim for multiple words. Let's see team words: olympus, mouse, triangle, horseshoe, film, canada, millionaire, pitch, state.

We need a clue that connects multiple. Maybe "green" connects "millionaire" (green maybe money), "field"? But field is opponent. Not good.

Maybe "silver" connects "horseshoe" (silver horseshoe), "millionaire" (silver? maybe not), "film" (silver screen), "state" (silver state = Nevada). Actually "silver screen" is film, "Silver State" is Nevada, "Silver horseshoe" is a thing, "Silver mouse"? maybe not, "Silver Olympic"? Not.

Clue must not be in any list. "Silver" is not listed. It could connect horseshoe, film, state. Also "silver" could connect "millionaire" (silver spoon? maybe). Could be okay.

Alternatively "Hollywood" connects film, millionaire (Hollywood stars), maybe not.

"North" could connect Canada, state (North state?), maybe not.

"Pitch" is a word. Could clue "black" connects "pitch" (black pitch), "millionaire"? maybe not. "Button" is opponent.

Maybe we choose clue "silver" with targets: horseshoe, film, state, millionaire? Use three targets to be safe: horseshoe (silver horseshoe), film (silver screen), state (Silver State = Nevada). That's three.

Check: clue word cannot be part of any target words. "Silver" not in any. Good.

Provide format:

CLUE: silver
TARGETS: horseshoe, film, state

That's valid
